# Koan Framework — agent orientation

Koan is an opinionated .NET 10 meta-framework for agentic, data-driven applications. Its target is
V0 to V1 in meaningful small steps: application code should read as business intent while framework
pillars own composition, backend negotiation, lifecycle, and explanation.

Status: 0.20 preview. Supported package owners share the 0.20 compatibility signal while package
patches remain independently owned. Other available packages are not promoted by proximity. Public-
feed publication follows the final package-only proof; docs/reference/product-surface.md is the
maturity authority.

## First supported action

The canonical package entry is:

    dotnet new install Sylin.Koan.Templates
    dotnet new koan-web -o TodoApi
    cd TodoApi
    dotnet run

Until the 0.20 package wave is observed on the public feed, run the proved checkout path:

    dotnet run --project samples/FirstUse

Then POST one approval to /api/approvals, read it back, and inspect
/.well-known/Koan/facts. Use the URL printed by ASP.NET Core if it differs.

The canonical web host is:

    var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
    builder.Services.AddKoan();
    var app = builder.Build();
    await app.RunAsync();

## Application grammar

- Entity<T> is the primary domain/data grammar.
- EntityController<T> is the ordinary HTTP projection.
- Instance and static Entity verbs are preferred over application repositories.
- References make capabilities available; AddKoan() compiles generated semantic modules once.
- A custom KoanModule is warranted only when an application or package owns real registration,
  startup work, or reporting. Its identity derives from normal package/assembly identity.
- Framework pillars own semantic policy; adapters own mechanics; applications own business intent.
- Do not introduce a new abstraction where a standard .NET convention or an existing pillar
  chokepoint owns the concern.

## Entity capability vocabulary

Data:

    await new Todo { Title = "Ship it" }.Save();
    var todo = await Todo.Get(id);
    var open = await Todo.Query(item => !item.Done);
    await todo.Remove();

Jobs:

    public sealed class Review : Entity<Review>, IKoanJob<Review>
    {
        public static Task Execute(Review review, JobContext context, CancellationToken ct) => ...;
    }
    await review.Job.Submit(ct: ct);

Communication:

    await order.Events.Raise<OrderApproved>(ct);
    await order.Transport.Send(ct);

Events mean something happened to an Entity. Transport means distribute an isolated copy of its
current state. Both work locally with no external adapter and lift pointwise over finite Entity
collections and lazy streams. Eligible connectors may extend the mesh without changing the terminal.

Agent projection:

    [McpEntity]
    public sealed class Todo : Entity<Todo>;

Referencing Koan.Mcp activates its KoanModule through AddKoan(). Ordinary applications do not call
AddKoanMcp() or map MCP endpoints manually.

## Truth and inspectability

- Read startup reporting before guessing about composition.
- Use /health/live for process liveness and /health/ready for dependency readiness.
- /.well-known/Koan/facts and koan://facts project the same redacted runtime decisions.
- koan://entities and koan://self describe the agent-visible surface.
- koan.lock.json records referenced-module composition and drift.
- Provider capability declarations bound what Koan may promise. Unsupported configured intent must
  reject with a corrective explanation rather than silently degrade.

## Documentation authority

Current product guidance is the root README, docs/toc.yml curriculum, graduated samples, current
reference pages, executable tests, and generated product surface. Initiative ledgers, assessments,
plans, proposals, and archives are engineering evidence, not alternate application patterns.
Architecture decision records are dated records and may describe their historical context.

Start reading at:

- README.md
- docs/getting-started/quickstart.md
- docs/getting-started/overview.md
- samples/README.md
- docs/architecture/product-constitution.md
- docs/architecture/entity-semantics-contract.md
- docs/reference/product-surface.md
